sections.paragraph:
m. תְּמִימָה f. adj. (from the root תָּמַם)-

(1) perfect, complete, Psa 19:8 Job 36:4 37:16 .

(2) whole, entire, Lev 3:9 25:30 Jos 10:13.

(3) perfect, whole, sound-(a) free from blemishes, used of victims, Exo 12:5 Lev 1:3.-(b) safe, secure, used of men, Pro 1:12.-(c) whole, upright in conduct, blameless, Gen 6:9 17:1 . תְּמִימֵי־דֶרֶךְ the upright of life (in the way), Psa 119:1. תָּמִים עִם אֵל upright towards God, i.e. altogether given to God, Deu 18:13 Psa 18:24 ( 2Sa 22:24, followed by לְ). Compare שָׁלֵם No. 3. Neutr. subst, integrity, Jos 24:14 Jud. 9:16 , 19 Jdg 9:19. So too הָלַךְ בְּתָמִים Psa 84:12, and הָלַךְ תָּמִים Psa 15:2, to walk (live) uprightly. 1Sa 14:41, הָבָה תָמִים “give the truth!”
